built.name: delphi-v0-llama2-12.8m
built.license_evidence: HF api cardData.license=mit + tag license:mit + README frontmatter 'license: mit'. No standalone LICENSE file (siblings: README.md only) — HF metadata is the authoritative license field for this repo.
built.quant: int4 group-wise (g=64, per-group fp16 scale + int8 zero), tie-aware; int8 variant also measured = 13.79 MB
built.runtime: karpathy llama2.c run.c (SentencePiece 4096-vocab tok); dequant -> .bin export
built.notes: LlamaForCausalLM hidden=384 layers=8 vocab=4096 tied. forward_ok=true, missing=0/unexpected=0, max dequant err ~0.022. Leaderboard est ~6.7MB; MEASURED 6.97MB (bundle honestly includes per-group scales/zeros + 64KB SentencePiece tokenizer + config). xz -tc integrity OK, 6 tar members.

built.name: delphi-v0-llama2-25.6m
built.license_evidence: HF api cardData.license=mit + tag license:mit. No standalone LICENSE file (siblings: README.md only) — HF metadata authoritative.
built.quant: int4 group-wise (g=64), tie-aware; int8 variant = 23.80 MB
built.notes: LlamaForCausalLM hidden=512 layers=8 vocab=4096 tied. forward_ok=true, missing=0/unexpected=0, max dequant err ~0.025. Leaderboard est ~11.7MB; MEASURED 12.70MB (scales/zeros + tokenizer + config overhead). xz integrity OK, 6 tar members.

built.name: delphi-v0-llama2-100k
built.license_evidence: HF api cardData.license=mit + tag license:mit. HF-metadata-only, no LICENSE file. Matches prior llm_tape_fit record.
built.notes: LlamaForCausalLM hidden=48 layers=4 vocab=4096 tied. forward_ok=true. 178KB on tape (~115KB int4 weights + 64KB SentencePiece tokenizer). Leaderboard est '147KB + tokenizer' -> consistent.

built.name: delphi-stories-llama2-50k
built.license_evidence: HF api cardData.license=apache-2.0 + tag license:apache-2.0. HF-metadata-only, no LICENSE file.
built.quant: int4 group-wise (g=64), NOT tied (config tie_word_embeddings=False, lm_head != embed -> both quantized separately); int8 = 0.096 MB
built.runtime: karpathy llama2.c / HF; uses tokenizer.json (HF fast, 154KB) — NOT SentencePiece
built.notes: LlamaForCausalLM hidden=6 layers=1 vocab=4096 UNTIED. forward_ok=true. 77KB on tape; tokenizer.json verified present in bundle. Leaderboard est ~178KB -> we BEAT it (49.5K params, tokenizer.json compresses well). 50k uses model.safetensors not pytorch_model.bin.

built.name: stories260K
built.license_evidence: karpathy/tinyllamas HF api cardData.license=mit + tag license:mit; karpathy llama2.c project is MIT on GitHub.
built.quant: int4 group-wise (g=64), tie-aware (output head shares tok_embeddings); int8 = 0.259 MB
built.runtime: karpathy llama2.c run.c + tok512.bin (512-vocab, 6KB) -> dequant to .bin export
built.notes: llama2.c native format (NOT a HF Llama checkpoint): dim=64 layers=5 vocab=512. .pt is a nanoGPT-style checkpoint (weights_only=False needed). all_finite=true, max dequant err ~0.106 (acceptable babble-tier). 130KB on tape (tiny 6KB tok512 vs 64KB SentencePiece). Leaderboard est 150KB int4 / 1.07MB FP32 -> we BEAT the int4 estimate.

built.name: delphi-v0-mamba-200k
built.license_evidence: HF api cardData.license=None, NO license:* tag (tags: transformers,pytorch,endpoints_compatible,region:us), README.md returns 'Entry not found', NO LICENSE file in siblings (config.json, pytorch_model.bin, tokenizer.* only). License unverifiable -> blocked per non-negotiable gate.
built.notes: BLOCKED — did NOT fetch weights or build a shippable artifact. Note: other delphi-suite Llama2 models carry MIT/Apache, but THIS mamba model specifically declares no license anywhere. Only a blocked-marker meta.json written.

built.name: chess-gpt-4.5M
built.license_evidence: HF API cardData.license='mit' AND README.md YAML front-matter 'license: mit'. No standalone LICENSE file (LICENSE/LICENSE.txt/LICENSE.md all 404), but the explicit HF license field + card front-matter is a definitive permissive tag.
built.quant: int4 (group=64, row-wise symmetric) headliner; int8 (group=128) also built at 6.261 MB on-tape
built.runtime: GPT2 forward (nanoGPT / llama2.c-style C runner). 32-token chess vocab carried in bundle (vocab.json). Tied embeddings (lm_head = wte^T). Runtime is a stub note, no full WASM built.
built.notes: GPT2LMHeadModel, 8L/n_embd=256/4 heads, vocab=32, 6.589M params. int4 on-tape 3.02 MB matches the ~3.2MB campaign estimate. int4 mean-rel quant error 0.169 (typical 4-bit at 256-dim); int8 near-lossless (relerr 0.011) at 6.26 MB if fidelity > size. Embeddings dominate (wte 32x256 + wpe 1024x256). Artifacts: chess_gpt_int4.bin.xz, chess_gpt_int8.bin.xz. Build scripts: payloads/built/build_chess_gpt.py + payloads/built/quantize_lib.py + per-dir build_chess-gpt-4.5M.sh.

built.name: chess_llms_8layer (adamkarvonen lichess_8layers)
built.license_evidence: HF API cardData.license=None (no tag). No LICENSE file (LICENSE/LICENSE.txt/LICENSE.md/license all 404). README.md has NO license statement (dataset/training notes only). The training-code repo github.com/adamkarvonen/train_ChessGPT IS MIT, but that licenses the CODE, not these weight checkpoints. adamkarvonen/chess_gpt_interpretability has NO license. No permissive license attaches to the weights.
built.notes: Intended Platinum headliner (25M lichess ChessGPT). BLOCKED: weights carry no permissive license. Fetched ONLY to measure: raw checkpoint 102.88 MB; xz -9e of raw .pt = 94.31 MB (compresses poorly: fp32 weights + dict metadata). NO shippable quantized artifact produced. The MIT training-code repo does not relicense the published weights. To unblock, the author must add an explicit permissive license tag to the chess_llms HF repo. meta.json + measure-only build_chess_llms_8layer.sh written.

built.name: othello-gpt
built.license_evidence: HF API cardData.license='mit' AND README.md YAML front-matter 'license: mit' on Baidicoot/Othello-GPT-Transformer-Lens. Chosen over awonga/* Othello repos (the prompt's hint - all have NO license tag) and over mntss/Othello-GPT (also MIT but ships redundant .pth files). NeelNanda/Othello-GPT-Transformer-Lens has NO license tag.
built.quant: int4 (group=64) size headliner 11.56 MB; int8 (group=128) fidelity option 24.03 MB. attn.mask causal buffers dropped (runtime regenerates).
built.runtime: minGPT forward pass. 61-token positional board vocab (no tokenizer file; squares 0-60). config.json carries dims. Causal mask regenerated at load. Runtime is a nanoGPT/minGPT forward stub, no full WASM built. torch.load uses weights_only=True (no arbitrary-code unpickle).
built.notes: Famous Othello world-model GPT (emergent board-state). minGPT 8L/d_model=512/8 heads/n_ctx=59/vocab=61, 25.34M params. int4 on-tape 11.56 MB but int4 mean-rel quant error 0.308 (wide weight dynamic range) risks degrading the learned board representation -- RECOMMEND int8 at 24.03 MB (relerr 0.033) as the fidelity-safe shippable headliner, int4 as the aggressive-size variant. Both round-trip OK. Artifacts: othello_int4.bin.xz, othello_int8.bin.xz. Build: payloads/built/build_othello.py + quantize_lib.py + per-dir build_othello-gpt.sh.

built.name: corpus_shakespeare
built.license_evidence: Project Gutenberg eBook #100 (Complete Works of William Shakespeare). Carries canonical 'Project Gutenberg eBook' PG-START/END markers (PD evidence). Shakespeare d.1616 — unambiguous public domain. Gutenberg distributes only PD/cleared texts.
built.runtime: plain UTF-8 text reader (any device); JSON index header
built.notes: 1 work (the PG compilation). Fetched live, boilerplate stripped, JSON index header prepended, xz -9e. On-disk .xz = 1668728 bytes. Round-trip decode verified, JSON index parses.

built.name: corpus_kjv_bible
built.license_evidence: Project Gutenberg eBook #10 (King James Version of the Holy Bible). KJV (1611) — public domain worldwide except UK crown-copyright which does not apply in the US. PG-START/END markers present.
built.runtime: plain UTF-8 text reader; JSON index header
built.notes: 1 work. On-disk .xz = 1048104 bytes. Smallest on-tape footprint of the set — compresses extremely well. Round-trip + JSON index verified.

built.name: corpus_sherlock_holmes
built.license_evidence: 9 Project Gutenberg eBooks comprising the FULL Holmes canon: 4 novels (#244 Study in Scarlet, #2097 Sign of Four, #2852 Hound, #3289 Valley of Fear) + 5 story collections (#1661 Adventures, #834 Memoirs, #108 Return, #2350 His Last Bow, #69700 Case-Book) = 60 works. All Doyle, all pre-1928 (last Case-Book stories 1921-1927), US public domain. Every file carries PG-START/END markers.
built.runtime: plain UTF-8 text reader; JSON index lists all 9 volumes
built.notes: 9 volumes = complete 60-work canon. On-disk .xz = 1070348 bytes. Each volume's title/author/sha256 in JSON index. Round-trip verified.

built.name: corpus_human_knowledge_starter
built.license_evidence: 18 Project Gutenberg eBooks, all pre-1928 US-PD, each with PG START/END markers verified (incl. #3300 Wealth of Nations, terse numeric-header form). Works: Darwin Origin(2009), Einstein Relativity(30155), Plato Republic(1497), Aurelius Meditations(2680), Sun Tzu(132), Federalist Papers(1404), Machiavelli Prince(1232), Smith Wealth of Nations(3300), Swift Modest Proposal(1080), Shelley Frankenstein(84), Shakespeare(100), Austen P&P(1342), Homer Odyssey(1727), Melville Moby-Dick(2701), Dickens Great Expectations(1400) & Tale of Two Cities(98), Twain Huck Finn(76), Carroll Alice(11).
built.runtime: plain UTF-8 text reader; JSON index with per-work title/author/sha256
built.source_url: https://www.gutenberg.org (18 PD works, ids in meta.json)
built.notes: 18 foundational works (science/philosophy/political-foundations/lit). On-disk .xz = 5073084 bytes. All PD-header verified true after fixing numeric-header detection. Round-trip + JSON index OK.

built.name: corpus_great_library
built.license_evidence: 69 unique Project Gutenberg eBooks (deduped from 75 entries), all pre-1928 US-PD, each PG START/END markers verified true. Spans world literature: Shakespeare, Austen x3, Dickens x5, Tolstoy (War&Peace 2600, Anna Karenina 1399), Dostoevsky x3, Melville, Twain x2, Bronte x2, Eliot, Hugo, Dumas x2, Joyce (Dubliners 2814, Ulysses 4300), Fitzgerald Great Gatsby(64317, 1925 PD), Verne x2, Wells x2, Kafka, Conrad, Poe, Du Bois Souls of Black Folk(408), plus philosophy/science from the starter set. All ids HTTP-probed live and confirmed pre-1928.
built.runtime: plain UTF-8 text reader; JSON index with all 69 works (title/author/sha256)
built.source_url: https://www.gutenberg.org (69 PD works, ids in meta.json)
built.notes: 69 PD classics (target ~58 exceeded after dedup). On-disk .xz = 17156612 bytes (17.16 MB). Largest corpus — likely needs multiple tape sides at current modem rates. All PD-header verified true. Round-trip + JSON index parse OK.

built.name: SmolLM2-135M-Instruct
built.license_evidence: HF API cardData.license = 'apache-2.0' (https://huggingface.co/api/models/HuggingFaceTB/SmolLM2-135M-Instruct). Apache-2.0 = permissive. No standalone LICENSE file in the repo tree, but the model card's explicit license field declares apache-2.0. Tokenizer (GPT-2 BPE) ships under the same terms.
built.quant: int4 (group-wise, group=64, fp16 per-group scale; every 2-D tensor incl. embed_tokens/lm_head quantized). Also built int8=130.658MB and aggressive int3=45.421MB.
built.runtime: transformers.js q4f16 WASM in-browser, or llama.cpp/llama2.c int4 dequant-on-load. NOT compiled to WASM here (path noted only).
built.notes: 'A cassette you can chat with.' int4=60.5MB (12% per-group rel err, safe for chat). Aggressive int3=45.4MB (28% rel err, roundtrips shape-exact, borderline coherence) is the smallest pack and is STILL 11.4MB OVER the ~34MB dream tier; int4 is 26.5MB over. NONE of the quants fit the ~34MB dream tier. Embeddings+lm_head (49152x576=28M of 134M params) dominate; reaching 34MB would need vocab pruning or int2 (not attempted). xz barely compresses (quantized weights ~random bytes). Model-level roundtrip confirmed: int4 AND int3 embed_tokens both dequantize to exact (49152,576), all finite. 272 tensors restored.

built.name: Piper TTS English voices (kathleen low + ljspeech medium)
built.license: MIT (repo weights) + per-voice dataset: CC0 (kathleen) / public-domain (ljspeech); espeak-ng frontend GPLv3
built.license_evidence: Repo cardData.license='mit'. The BINDING gate is each voice's training-DATASET license in its MODEL_CARD: VERIFIED en_US/kathleen/low -> 'CC0' (public-domain dedication), en_US/ljspeech/medium -> 'public domain' (keithito LJ-Speech). Both ship-clear permissive. The .onnx voice WEIGHTS are MIT+permissive-dataset. BUT Piper's runtime phonemizer frontend (espeak-ng) is GPLv3 -> if bundled it's gpl_with_source (ship source like DOOM side B); hence the gpl_with_source status flag. Deliberately AVOIDED the NC-trap voices: ryan + hfc_male/hfc_female = CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (non-commercial, BLOCKED), lessac = CSTR Blizzard custom license, amy/danny = Mycroft 'See URL' (unverified).
built.quant: bundle (fp32 ONNX as-shipped, xz -9e). No int8 quant applied: no onnxruntime in env to validate a quantized ONNX graph, so weights left native to avoid shipping a corrupt quant. int8 dynamic quant would ~halve each voice to ~30MB but is UNVERIFIED here.
built.runtime: onnxruntime-web (WASM) for VITS inference + espeak-ng (GPLv3) phonemizer frontend. NOT run here (no onnxruntime in env) — fetched + sized + license-verified only.
built.notes: 'A cassette that talks back.' HONEST x_low note: the current rhasspy/piper-voices repo has NO x_low tier for ANY English voice (smallest English = 'low'/16kHz); used kathleen 'low' (CC0) as x_low-equivalent + ljspeech 'medium' (PD). Per voice ~58MB on tape (xz barely compresses fp32 float weights); both = 116MB. Way over any tape tier as native ONNX; int8 ONNX quant (~30MB/voice) is the unlock but needs onnxruntime to validate (absent). Runtime=onnxruntime-web. License status flagged gpl_with_source because the espeak-ng inference frontend is GPLv3 even though the weights are MIT+CC0/PD.

built.name: TinyCode-python
built.license_evidence: HF API cardData.license = 'mit' (https://huggingface.co/api/models/blueapple8259/TinyCode-python). MIT = permissive. No standalone LICENSE file in repo tree, but the model card's explicit license field declares MIT. Tokenizer (GPT-2-style BPE) ships with the model.
built.quant: int4 (group-wise, group=64, fp16 per-group scale; UNTIED embeddings so both embed_tokens AND lm_head quantized). Also built int8=28.424MB.
built.runtime: transformers.js q4f16 WASM in-browser, or llama.cpp/llama2.c int4 dequant-on-load. NOT compiled to WASM here (path noted only).
built.notes: 'A tape that writes code.' int4=12.3MB on tape — comfortably FITS the working/dream tiers (well under ~34MB). int8=28.4MB if a tier allows higher fidelity. Arch is tiny hidden=128/4-layers but huge vocab=52000 with intermediate_size=11008, so embeddings + one fat MLP dominate; int4 rel err 13% acceptable for a code-completion toy. Source is fp32 (4 bytes/param) -> 121MB raw for only ~30M params. Model-level roundtrip confirmed: untied lm_head quantized, embed shape (52000,128) exact, tokenizer+config bundled in the tar (39 tensors).

manifest.summary: Curated the consolidated BUILT-PAYLOADS MANIFEST across all built payloads in /Users/magnus/repos/cassette-ai/payloads/built/ on branch exp/bps-push-2026-06-14. 22 payloads total: 19 SHIP-CLEAR/gpl_with_source and built (all on-disk byte sizes verified to match agent reports exactly — the agents were honest), 2 BLOCKED (no license: delphi-v0-mamba-200k, adamkarvonen chess_llms_8layer), 1 build INCOMPLETE (piper-en-voices: license-verified + fetched but no .xz tape artifact written — its 116 MB is projected, not measured). My RESULTS JSON covered 17 payloads (TinyStories LMs, board-game models, PD corpora, frontier/stretch); disk additionally holds 5 fully-built shippable payloads from sibling clusters (chip8_octo, tic80, v86_linux, ddpm-mnist, learned-planner-drc33) which I verified and folded in. Tier-ordered manifest below carries only measured on_tape_mb for genuinely-built artifacts. License gate enforced: 2 weights with no permissive tag stayed blocked; 2 GPL-component bundles (v86_linux, piper) flagged gpl_with_source.

built_manifest.note: 'A cassette you can chat with.' NONE of int3/int4/int8 fit the ~34MB dream tier (int3 still 11.4MB over). Embeddings+lm_head (28M of 134M params) dominate; xz barely compresses quantized weights

built_manifest.name: Piper TTS English voices (kathleen low + ljspeech medium)
built_manifest.quant: none applied (fp32 ONNX as-shipped; no onnxruntime in env to validate a quantized graph)
built_manifest.runtime: onnxruntime-web WASM VITS + espeak-ng (GPLv3) phonemizer
built_manifest.note: HONEST GAP: no .xz tape artifact on disk — only two raw .onnx (126.64MB measured). The 116.018MB figure is PROJECTED, not measured. License-verified + fetched + sized only. gpl_with_source (espeak-ng frontend GPLv3). int8 ONNX quant (~30MB/voice) is the unlock but needs onnxruntime (absent)

blocked.name: delphi-v0-mamba-200k
blocked.why: HF cardData.license=None, NO license:* tag, README.md returns 'Entry not found', NO LICENSE file in siblings. License unverifiable -> blocked per non-negotiable gate. Sibling delphi-suite Llama2 models carry MIT/Apache but THIS mamba model declares no license anywhere. Only a blocked-marker meta.json written; no weights fetched.
blocked.how_to_unblock: Author (delphi-suite) adds an explicit permissive license tag (MIT/Apache-2.0) to the v0-mamba-200k HF repo, matching their other models. Then re-run build_delphi-v0-mamba-200k from fetch.

blocked.name: chess_llms_8layer (adamkarvonen lichess_8layers)
blocked.why: Intended Platinum headliner (25M lichess ChessGPT). cardData.license=None, no LICENSE file, README has no license statement. The MIT training-code repo (github.com/adamkarvonen/train_ChessGPT) licenses the CODE, not these published weight checkpoints; chess_gpt_interpretability also has no license. No permissive license attaches to the weights. Fetched ONLY to measure (raw .pt 102.88MB on disk under src/; xz -9e of raw = 94.31MB, compresses poorly). NO shippable quantized artifact produced.
blocked.how_to_unblock: Author adds an explicit permissive license tag to the adamkarvonen/chess_llms HF repo. Then quantize int4 (est ~12MB) and build. Until then, ship the MIT-clear derickio/chess-gpt-4.5M as the board-game LM instead.

manifest.size_vs_estimate: All on-disk byte sizes were independently re-measured and match the agent-reported on_tape_mb exactly to the byte (e.g. delphi-12.8m 7311572 B, othello int4 11558568 B, corpus_great 17156612 B) — no faked sizes. Material divergences from the leaderboard ESTIMATES: (1) UNDER-estimate wins — delphi-stories-50k beat ~178KB est, landing 77KB (tokenizer.json compresses well); stories260K beat its int4 est at 130KB (tiny 6KB tok512 vs 64KB SentencePiece); ddpm-mnist beat ~2MB est at 0.99MB int8; learned-planner beat ~1.3MB at 0.79MB; v86_linux landed 2.54MB vs 5.5MB target (wasm + ISO both compress hard). (2) OVER-estimate honesty — delphi 12.8m/25.6m came in slightly above the leaderboard's bare-weight estimates (6.97->7.31MB, 11.7->12.70MB) because the bundles honestly include per-group int4 scales/zeros + the SentencePiece tokenizer + config, which the estimates omitted. (3) STRETCH tier confirmed over-budget — SmolLM2-135M does NOT fit the ~34MB dream tier at ANY quant (int3=45.4MB is the floor, still 11.4MB over; xz barely compresses quantized weights, which are near-random bytes). (4) MEASUREMENT GAP — Piper's 116MB is PROJECTED not measured (no .xz on disk; raw onnx sum is 126.64MB); a real xz pass and/or int8 ONNX quant is needed before it can carry a measured size.
next_actions: FINISH PIPER BUILD: write the actual xz tape artifact (xz -9e the onnx+config bundle) so on_tape_mb is MEASURED not projected; then attempt int8 dynamic ONNX quant (~30MB/voice) once onnxruntime is available, and validate the quantized graph before shipping.
COMMIT (lead, not parallel agents): git add the tracked meta.json + build_*.sh + *.md across all payloads/built/<name>/ on branch exp/bps-push-2026-06-14; the built/** binaries stay gitignored.

UNBLOCK requests: file no-license findings upstream — delphi-suite/v0-mamba-200k and adamkarvonen/chess_llms both need an explicit permissive license tag before they can ship. Until then substitute the MIT-clear derickio/chess-gpt-4.5M for the board-game LM slot.

OTHELLO SHIP DECISION: choose int8 (24.03MB, relerr 0.033) as the fidelity-safe headliner over int4 (11.56MB, relerr 0.308) to preserve the learned board-state representation; document the size/fidelity tradeoff in the dossier.

DOUBLE-SIDE the over-budget payloads: corpus_great_library (17.16MB) and SmolLM2-135M (45-60MB) exceed single-side capacity at current modem rates — plan a multi-side encode or drop SmolLM2 from the shippable set and keep it as a documented stretch result.

RECONCILE estimates: update the leaderboard so model bundle estimates include int4 scales/zeros + tokenizer + config overhead (the source of the 12.8m/25.6m over-estimate gap), preventing future under-budgeting.
